api.tests module

class api.tests.APITests(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: APITestCase

API test suite for the newsapplication.

Covers: - Article creation by journalists - Article approval and deletion by editors - Newsletter-article addition - Permissions enforcement for readers - Reader subscription articles - Email notifications via signals

setUp()[source]

Setup test users, publisher, articles, and newsletter.

Creates: - A reader, editor, journalist, and publisher owner - A publisher instance - Two articles - A newsletter

test_add_article_to_newsletter()[source]

Test adding an article to a newsletter.

Verifies that the article count in the newsletter increases.

test_editor_can_approve_article()[source]

Test that an editor can approve an article by updating the ‘approved’ field.

Expects HTTP 200 OK on successful update.

test_editor_can_delete_article()[source]

Test that an editor can delete an article.

Expects HTTP 200 OK on successful deletion.

test_journalist_can_create_articles()[source]

Test that a journalist can create a new article via the API.

Expects HTTP 201 CREATED on successful submission.

test_reader_cannot_create_article()[source]

Test that a reader cannot create an article.

Expects HTTP 403 FORBIDDEN as readers are not allowed to create articles.

test_reader_retrieve_subscribed_content()[source]

Test that a reader can retrieve articles from their subscribed journalists and publishers.

  • Marks article1 as published.

  • Subscribes reader to a journalist and publisher.

  • Expects HTTP 200 OK with articles returned.

test_signal_sending_email_to_subscribers()[source]

Test that the signal sends an email when an article is published.

  • Ensures that only subscribed readers receive notifications.

api.urls module

api.views module

api.views.article(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Retrieve a single article by primary key.

Authentication:

  • JWTAuthentication required.

Parameters:

pk – Primary key of the article.

Returns:

Serialized article data or 404 if not found.

api.views.article_create(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Create a new article.

Rules:

  • Only users with role “Journalist” may create articles.

  • Journalists cannot create articles on behalf of others.

  • The provided author must also have role “Journalist”.

Method:

  • POST only.

Returns:

  • 201 CREATED on success.

  • 400 BAD REQUEST for validation errors.

  • 403 FORBIDDEN for permission violations.

api.views.article_delete(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Delete an article.

Rules:

  • Only users with role “Journalist” and “Editor” may delete articles.

  • An article may only be deleted by its author.

Parameters:

pk – Primary key of the article to delete.

Returns:

  • 200 OK if deleted.

  • 403 FORBIDDEN if permission denied.

  • 404 NOT FOUND if article does not exist.

api.views.article_update(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Update an existing article.

Permissions: - User must be authenticated. - Only a Journalist and Editor may update the article.

Parameters:

  • request (HttpRequest) – The HTTP request object.

  • pk (int) – Primary key of the article to update.

Returns:

  • 200 OK with updated article data.

  • 403 Forbidden if user is not the author.

  • 404 Not Found if article does not exist.

  • 400 Bad Request if validation fails.

Return type:

Response

api.views.articles(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Retrieve all published articles.

Authentication:

  • JWTAuthentication required.

Method:

  • GET only.

Returns:

  • 200 OK with serialized list of published articles.

api.views.subscriber_articles(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Retrieve published articles from a reader’s subscriptions.

This endpoint returns articles that:

  • Are marked as published.

  • Are authored by journalists the reader subscribes to.

  • OR are published by publishers the reader subscribes to.

Access Rules:

  • Only users with role “Reader” may access this endpoint.

  • The reader must exist in the system.

Response Codes:

  • 200 OK: Successfully returns serialized articles.

  • 403 FORBIDDEN: If the requesting user is not a Reader.

  • 404 NOT FOUND: If the reader has no subscriptions.

Parameters:

  • request – The HTTP request object.

  • pk – Primary key of the reader whose subscriptions are being queried.

Returns:

JSON response containing serialized articles or error message.
